heading: Biomass research and development

children.label: A
children.text: an industrial product (including chemicals, materials, and polymers) produced from biomass;

children.label: B
children.text: a commercial or industrial product (including animal feed and electric power) derived in connection with the conversion of biomass to fuel; or

children.label: i
children.text: is intended for permanent sequestration or utilization; and

children.label: ii
children.text: is a byproduct of the production of the products described in subparagraphs (A) and (B).

children.heading: Demonstration
children.label: 2 Demonstration
children.text: The term “demonstration” means demonstration of technology in a pilot plant or semi-works scale facility, including a plant or facility located on a farm.

children.heading: Initiative
children.label: 3 Initiative
children.text: The term “Initiative” means the Biomass Research and Development Initiative established under subsection (e).

children.heading: Cooperation and coordination in biomass research and development
children.label: b Cooperation and coordination in biomass research and development

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Energy shall coordinate policies and procedures that promote research and development regarding the production of biofuels and biobased products.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture shall designate, as the point of contact for the Department of Agriculture, an officer of the Department of Agriculture appointed by the President to a position in the Department before the date of the designation, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of Energy shall designate, as the point of contact for the Department of Energy, an officer of the Department of Energy appointed by the President to a position in the Department before the date of the designation, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate.

children.heading: Biomass Research and Development Board
children.label: c Biomass Research and Development Board

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established the Biomass Research and Development Board to carry out the duties described in paragraph (3).

children.label: A
children.text: the point of contacts of the Department of Energy and the Department of Agriculture, who shall serve as cochairpersons of the Board;

children.label: B
children.text: a senior officer of each of the Department of the Interior, the Environmental Protection Agency, the National Science Foundation, and the Office of Science and Technology Policy, each of whom shall have a rank that is equivalent to the rank of the points of contact; and

children.label: C
children.text: at the option of the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of Energy, other members appointed by the Secretaries (after consultation with the Board).

children.label: i
children.text: between the Department of Agriculture and the Department of Energy; and

children.label: ii
children.text: with other departments and agencies of the Federal Government;

children.label: B
children.text: provide recommendations to the points of contact concerning administration of this chapter;

children.label: i
children.text: solicitations are open and competitive with awards made annually; and

children.label: ii
children.text: objectives and evaluation criteria of the solicitations are clearly stated and minimally prescriptive, with no areas of special interest; and

children.label: D
children.text: ensure that the panel of scientific and technical peers assembled under subsection (e) to review proposals is composed predominantly of independent experts selected from outside the Departments of Agriculture and Energy.

children.heading: Funding
children.label: 4 Funding
children.text: Each agency represented on the Board is encouraged to provide funds for any purpose under this section.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 5 Meetings
children.text: The Board shall meet at least quarterly.

children.heading: Biomass Research and Development Technical Advisory Committee
children.label: d Biomass Research and Development Technical Advisory Committee

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: There is established the Biomass Research and Development Technical Advisory Committee to carry out the duties described in paragraph (3).

children.label: i
children.text: an individual affiliated with the biofuels industry;

children.label: ii
children.text: an individual affiliated with the biobased industrial and commercial products industry;

children.label: iii
children.text: an individual affiliated with an institution of higher education who has expertise in biofuels and biobased products;

children.label: iv
children.text: 2 prominent engineers or scientists from government or academia who have expertise in biofuels and biobased products;

children.label: v
children.text: an individual affiliated with a commodity trade association;

children.label: vi
children.text: 2 individuals affiliated with environmental or conservation organizations;

children.label: vii
children.text: an individual associated with State government who has expertise in biofuels and biobased products;

children.label: viii
children.text: an individual with expertise in energy and environmental analysis;

children.label: ix
children.text: an individual with expertise in the economics of biofuels and biobased products;

children.label: x
children.text: an individual with expertise in agricultural economics;

children.label: xi
children.text: an individual with expertise in plant biology and biomass feedstock development;

children.label: xii
children.text: an individual with expertise in agronomy, crop science, or soil science;

children.label: xiii
children.text: an individual with expertise in carbon dioxide capture, utilization, and sequestration; and

children.label: xiv
children.text: at the option of the points of contact, other members.

children.heading: Appointment
children.label: B Appointment
children.text: The members of the Advisory Committee shall be appointed by the points of contact.

children.label: A
children.text: advise the points of contact with respect to the Initiative; and

children.label: i
children.text: funds authorized for the Initiative are distributed and used in a manner that is consistent with the objectives, purposes, and considerations of the Initiative;

children.label: ii
children.text: solicitations are open and competitive with awards made annually;

children.label: iii
children.text: objectives and evaluation criteria of the solicitations are clearly stated and minimally prescriptive, with no areas of special interest;

children.label: iv
children.text: the points of contact are funding proposals under this chapter that are selected on the basis of merit, as determined by an independent panel of scientific and technical peers predominantly from outside the Departments of Agriculture and Energy; and

children.label: v
children.text: activities under this chapter are carried out in accordance with this chapter.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: 4 Coordination
children.text: To avoid duplication of effort, the Advisory Committee shall coordinate its activities with those of other Federal advisory committees working in related areas.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 5 Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall meet at least quarterly.

children.heading: Terms
children.label: 6 Terms
children.text: Members of the Advisory Committee shall be appointed for a term of 3 years.

children.heading: Biomass Research and Development Initiative
children.label: e Biomass Research and Development Initiative
A
children.text: biofuels and biobased products; and

children.label: B
children.text: the methods, practices, and technologies, for the production of biofuels and biobased products.

children.label: A
children.text: technologies and processes necessary for abundant commercial production of biofuels at prices competitive with fossil fuels;

children.label: i
children.text: to enhance the economic viability of biofuels and power;

children.label: ii
children.text: to serve as substitutes for petroleum-based feedstocks and products;

children.label: iii
children.text: to enhance the value of coproducts produced using the technologies and processes; and

children.label: iv
children.text: to permanently sequester or utilize carbon dioxide described in subsection (a)(1)(C); and

children.label: C
children.text: a diversity of economically and environmentally sustainable domestic sources of renewable biomass for conversion to biofuels, bioenergy, and biobased products.

children.heading: Feedstocks development
children.label: A Feedstocks development
children.text: Research, development, and demonstration activities regarding feedstocks and feedstock logistics (including the harvest, handling, transport, preprocessing, and storage) relevant to production of raw materials for conversion to biofuels and biobased products.

children.heading: Biofuels and biobased products development
children.label: B Biofuels and biobased products development
i
children.text: the development of diverse cost-effective technologies for the use of cellulosic biomass in the production of biofuels and biobased products;

children.label: ii
children.text: product diversification through technologies relevant to production of a range of biobased products (including chemicals, animal feeds, and cogenerated power) that potentially can increase the feasibility of fuel production in a biorefinery; and

children.label: iii
children.text: the development of technologies to permanently sequester or utilize carbon dioxide described in subsection (a)(1)(C).

children.heading: Strategic guidance
children.label: i Strategic guidance
children.text: The development of analysis that provides strategic guidance for the application of renewable biomass technologies to improve sustainability and environmental quality, cost effectiveness, security, and rural economic development.

children.heading: Energy and environmental impact
children.label: ii Energy and environmental impact
children.text: Development of systematic evaluations of the impact of expanded biofuel production on the environment (including forest land) and on the food supply for humans and animals, including the improvement and development of tools for life cycle analysis of current and potential biofuels.

children.heading: Assessment of Federal land
children.label: iii Assessment of Federal land
children.text: Assessments of the potential of Federal land resources to increase the production of feedstocks for biofuels and biobased products, consistent with the integrity of soil and water resources and with other environmental considerations.

children.label: A
children.text: to create continuously expanding opportunities for participants in existing biofuels production by seeking synergies and continuity with current technologies and practices;

children.label: B
children.text: to maximize the environmental, economic, and social benefits of production of biofuels and derived biobased products on a large scale; and

children.label: C
children.text: to facilitate small-scale production and local and on-farm use of biofuels, including the development of small-scale gasification technologies for production of biofuel from cellulosic feedstocks.

children.label: A
children.text: an institution of higher education;

children.label: B
children.text: a National Laboratory;

children.label: C
children.text: a Federal research agency;

children.label: D
children.text: a State research agency;

children.label: E
children.text: a private sector entity;

children.label: F
children.text: a nonprofit organization; or

children.label: G
children.text: a consortium of 2 or more entities described in subparagraphs (A) through (F).

children.label: i
children.text: publish annually 1 or more joint requests for proposals for grants, contracts, and assistance under this subsection;

children.label: ii
children.text: require that grants, contracts, and assistance under this section be awarded based on a scientific peer review by an independent panel of scientific and technical peers;

children.label: I
children.text: involve a consortia of experts from multiple institutions;

children.label: II
children.text: encourage the integration of disciplines and application of the best technical resources; and

children.label: III
children.text: increase the geographic diversity of demonstration projects; and

children.label: iv
children.text: require that the technical areas described in each of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (3) receive not less than 15 percent of funds made available to carry out this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Except as provided in subclause (II), the non-Federal share of the cost of a research or development project under this section shall be not less than 20 percent.

children.heading: Reduction
children.label: II Reduction
children.text: The Secretary of Agriculture or the Secretary of Energy, as appropriate, may reduce the non-Federal share required under subclause (I) if the appropriate Secretary determines the reduction to be necessary and appropriate.

children.heading: Demonstration and commercial projects
children.label: ii Demonstration and commercial projects
children.text: The non-Federal share of the cost of a demonstration or commercial project under this section shall be not less than 50 percent.

children.label: i
children.text: adapted, made available, and disseminated, as appropriate; and

children.label: ii
children.text: included in the best practices database established under section 5925e(e) of this title.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Energy and the Secretary of Agriculture may provide such administrative support and funds of the Department of Energy and the Department of Agriculture to the Board and the Advisory Committee as are necessary to enable the Board and the Advisory Committee to carry out their duties under this section.

children.heading: Other agencies
children.label: 2 Other agencies
children.text: The heads of the agencies referred to in subsection (c)(2)(B), and the other members of the Board appointed under subsection (c)(2)(C), are encouraged to provide administrative support and funds of their respective agencies to the Board and the Advisory Committee.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 3 Limitation
children.text: Not more than 4 percent of the amount made available for each fiscal year under subsection (h) may be used to pay the administrative costs of carrying out this section.

the status and progress of the Initiative, including a report from the Advisory Committee on whether funds appropriated for the Initiative have been distributed and used in a manner that is consistent with the objectives and requirements of this section;
the general status of cooperation and research and development efforts carried out at each agency with respect to biofuels and biobased products; and
the plans of the Secretary of Energy and the Secretary of Agriculture for addressing concerns raised in the report, including concerns raised by the Advisory Committee.

children.label: A
children.text: $20,000,000 for fiscal year 2009;

children.label: B
children.text: $28,000,000 for fiscal year 2010;

children.label: C
children.text: $30,000,000 for fiscal year 2011;

children.label: D
children.text: $40,000,000 for fiscal year 2012; and

children.label: E
children.text: $3,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2017.

children.heading: Discretionary funding
children.label: 2 Discretionary funding
children.text: In addition to any other funds made available to carry out this section, there is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section $20,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2014 through 2023.
